methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
T1 Energy, a public-stage Climate & Energy company that has raised over $1B, operates within a fragmented sector comprising 21 identifiable peers—1 public company and 8 private firms with meaningful capital deployment. Top private comparables include QuantumScape, LanzaTech, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion; the lone public peer is LanzaTech (LNZA).

A peer-median valuation method proved inapplicable to this analysis. The narrow peer set—filtered by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints necessary for statistical reliability. Specifically, two same-stage peers were identified, zero adjacent-stage comparables, and zero similar-tier companies, falling short of the three-point minimum threshold. Expanding to all 21 Climate & Energy sector peers would introduce substantial methodological noise given the heterogeneity of business models, technology readiness, and capital intensity across the broader sector.

The absence of a sufficiently dense comparable universe limits triangulation-based valuation precision for T1 Energy. Practitioners would need to rely on alternative methods—discounted cash flow analysis, precedent transaction analysis, or qualitative stage-and-sector benchmarking—to establish a defensible valuation range. Key uncertainties center on technology maturation risk, commercialization timeline, and the degree to which T1 Energy's battery chemistry and manufacturing approach command premium or discount multiples relative to the sparse public and private peer set.
